summary: PR #2334 replaces the placeholder for `fn_800DC070` in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.c` with a small ftCommon CaptureJump enter function. The implementation gets the fighter, saves `fp->victim_gobj`, resets common state, sets self velocity from `p_ftCommonData->x374/x378`, clears `mv.co.buryjump.x0`, calls `ftCo_800DC920`, and transitions to `ftCo_MS_CaptureJump`. The PR body claims `fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0`, but no objdiff/checkdiff/build log or review comments are present in the slice.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; replaces `/// #fn_800DC070` with the recovered CaptureJump enter function.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary and What this function does

what_changed.text: Removed the `/// #fn_800DC070` placeholder and added `void fn_800DC070(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`.
Introduced local `Fighter* fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` and `Fighter_GObj* victim = fp->victim_gobj` before calling `ftCommon_8007D5D4(fp)`.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: What this function does items 1-2

what_changed.text: Set `fp->self_vel.x` to `-fp->facing_dir * p_ftCommonData->x374`, `fp->self_vel.y` to `p_ftCommonData->x378`, and reset `fp->mv.co.buryjump.x0` to `0.0F`.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: What this function does items 3-4

what_changed.text: Called `ftCo_800DC920(victim, gobj)` and then `Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, ftCo_MS_CaptureJump, 0, 0.0F, 1.0F, 0.0F, NULL)`.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: What this function does items 5-6

smart_moves.text: Saved `fp->victim_gobj` into a local `victim` before the common-state reset call, preserving the object needed by the later `ftCo_800DC920(victim, gobj)` call.
Used existing typed fighter fields and symbols (`self_vel`, `facing_dir`, `mv.co.buryjump.x0`, `p_ftCommonData`, `ftCo_MS_CaptureJump`) rather than raw offset access.
Kept the implementation as direct C with existing project helpers and no visible pragmas, inline assembly, padding, or dummy storage tactics.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For small enter-state fighter functions, a reviewable match can often be expressed as the natural call/field sequence: get `Fighter`, preserve any object pointer needed after state-reset helpers, update velocity/state fields, then call the motion-state transition helper.
When a later helper needs a field value that may be conceptually tied to the pre-reset state, store it in a named local before calling the reset/helper function.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `Fighter_GObj* victim = fp->victim_gobj;` before `ftCommon_8007D5D4(fp)` and later `ftCo_800DC920(victim, gobj)`

naming_conventions.text: Used conventional short fighter local `fp` for `Fighter*` returned by `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)`.
Used semantic local name `victim` for `fp->victim_gobj`, matching the PR body's explanation that this object is the escaping fighter.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Preserve needed fighter object fields before reset helpers
accepted_candidate_records.text: In `fn_800DC070`, the implementation copies `fp->victim_gobj` into `victim` before `ftCommon_8007D5D4(fp)` and then uses that saved object in `ftCo_800DC920(victim, gobj)`. This is a narrow candidate lesson for fighter state-transition functions where a reset/helper call precedes later use of an object pointer from the original fighter state.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use `fp` and semantic object locals when directly supported
accepted_candidate_records.text: The diff uses `fp` for the `Fighter*` from `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` and `victim` for `fp->victim_gobj`; the latter is supported by both the field name and PR body description.
